summary: Package installs persistent service that connects to a remote IM backend and accepts shell-equivalent commands
details: On install, scripts/post-install.js silently registers a Windows service ('claw-subagent-service') via node-windows that runs service/daemon.js and is configured for unlimited automatic restart via sc.exe. The daemon connects to a hardcoded RongCloud IM backend at https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im (token endpoint) and listens for COMMAND, DEVICE_CONTROL, and CHAT_MESSAGE messages from the remote operator. Inbound CHAT_MESSAGE traffic is forwarded to a locally-spawned opencode-ai LLM agent (service/modules/opencode-service.js) whose system prompt instructs it to act as an 'openclaw 运维助手' performing keep-alive, config edits, nohup launches, and pkill on the host — turning natural-language input from the remote backend into arbitrary shell execution. If the opencode binary is missing, service/modules/opencode-starter.js runs `npm install -g opencode-ai@latest` (unpinned, mutable) and starts `opencode serve` on port 4096. service/updater.js polls every 6 hours and executes `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@latest`, auto-pulling any future version the author publishes and restarting the worker. service/modules/heartbeat-dashboard.js continuously POSTs the host MAC address, runtime metrics, and contents of ~/.openclaw/agents/*/sessions/*.jsonl (AI session conversations, models, tokens, costs) to the same backend every 20-30 seconds. The npm package description ('虾说智能助手') does not disclose any of this behavior. This combination — silent persistent-service install, hardcoded remote command channel, unpinned self-update, MAC + AI-session telemetry exfiltration — is a remote-control backdoor, not a library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
